scenario_title: Tracking star positions in a constellation
scenario_description: Astronomy: Tracking star positions in a constellation

turns.text: Here's a photo of the night sky I took last week. Can you tell me about the constellation's star positions?
turns.image_description: Night sky view of a constellation with stars in their standard positions. Clear sky, no clouds.

turns.text: I see the standard configuration of the constellation here. The stars are in their typical alignment for this time of year. Would you like me to compare this with subsequent observations?
Sure, here's the same constellation one night later. I noticed a slight shift in one of the stars.
turns.image_description: One night later: One star has shifted position slightly due to atmospheric distortion (distractor).

turns.text: The shift you mentioned appears to be minimal and could be caused by atmospheric distortion. Let me check your next observation to see if this change persists.
Here's the view two nights later. The star has returned to its original position, but there's a new star visible now.
turns.image_description: Two nights later: The same star has returned to its original position. A new star is visible (distractor).

turns.text: The star has indeed returned to its original position, which suggests the earlier shift was temporary. The new star might be an artifact of the camera or a different celestial object. Let me review your latest image.

What is the current alignment of the constellation's stars?

B) Original star positions
A) Slight shift in one star
C) A new star visible
D) Partially cloudy sky

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 established the baseline of standard star positions. Turn 2 introduced a distractor (atmospheric distortion), which was dismissed in Turn 4. Turn 3 added another distractor (new star), which was acknowledged but not confirmed. Turn 4 showed all stars back to original positions with clouds, confirming the baseline state.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: Distractors in turns 2 and 3 required ignoring transient atmospheric effects and camera artifacts to confirm the true alignment.

_meta.scenario: Astronomy: Tracking star positions in a constellation
